description: In an apartment living room, Joey sits on a stool by a kitchen counter. He wears a blue striped shirt. A phone rings audibly. He reaches across the counter, picks up a black cordless phone, and presses a button.

Joey brings the phone to his ear and says, "Hello?" Over the phone, Chandler says, "Hey, it's me. I know you can't stand to be in the same room as me so I just thought I'd try and apologize..." Joey's eyes move side to side.
Chandler's voice continues, "...over the phone all." Joey pulls the phone away from his ear, presses a button, and sets it down on the counter.
Joey leans his forearms onto the counter. The phone rings audibly. Joey looks at the phone.
Joey picks up the phone, presses a button, and brings it to his ear, his mouth moving briefly.
Over the phone, Chandler says, "Look, I never should have kissed your girlfriend and I'm"
Joey swiftly pulls the phone from his ear and slams it onto the counter. He sits back on the stool.
The phone rings again. Joey picks it up and yells loudly, "it. Stop calling." Chandler's voice, speaking rapidly over the phone, responds, "Hey. Hey."
Chandler's fast-paced voice continues over the phone, "This is 92.3 WXR KRA calling with our $1,000 daily challenge." Joey's facial expression relaxes.
Chandler's voice adds, "All right," and Joey smiles.
Joey maintains his smile while listening.
Chandler's voice over the phone asks, "what is the name of your roommate who is very, very sorry and would do anything". Joey's smile drops and his facial muscles tighten.
Joey lowers the phone, presses a button, and slams it onto the counter again. He rests his hand on his thigh.
Joey leans his forearms on the counter. The phone rings. Joey grabs the phone and lifts it.
Joey speaks loudly into the phone, "damn it, Chandler, stop calling."
Over the phone, Ross says, "Joey. It's Ross." Joey's facial expression relaxes slightly.
Joey lowers his volume and says, "Oh, sorry, man." Ross replies over the phone, "It's all right."
Joey says, "What's up?" Ross replies over the phone, "Not much."
Joey holds the phone to his ear.
Ross says over the phone, "I got Chandler here, and he's really, really". Joey closes his eyes.
The screen turns completely black. No audio is present.
The screen remains black in silence.
The setting changes to a different apartment kitchen and living area. Chandler walks through an open door. Monica, Phoebe, Ross, and Rachel are present in the room. Chandler exhales audibly.
Chandler stops beside a kitchen island basket and says, "turkey."
Chandler leans on the island. Phoebe stands next to the basket and says, "Oh, giving thanks."
Chandler exhales heavily, closing his eyes briefly. Phoebe places her hands on the basket.
Phoebe smiles and says, "Look, everyone, it's the spirit of Thanksgiving."
Rachel walks forward holding a mug. Chandler turns toward the refrigerator.
Rachel asks, "Are things with you and Joey any better?" Chandler turns toward her and replies, "They couldn't be worse."
Chandler continues, "I spent 8 hours calling him last night just trying to get him to talk to" while walking toward the refrigerator.
Chandler points to his chest as he finishes, "me." Rachel sits on the back of the sofa and says, "Oh, wow, 8 hours."
Rachel gestures with her free hand and adds, "So you could probably really use one of those plug in telephone, huh?"
Ross looks at Rachel and says, "Should we all expect Christmas gifts that can be stolen from your office?" Monica stirs a bowl beside him.
Rachel looks at Ross.
Rachel points her index finger at Ross and says, "You shouldn't."
Ross lowers his gaze to the counter and begins chopping a celery stick with a knife.
Phoebe gestures with both hands and says, "Speaking of Christmas since Monica and I are starting a new business".
Phoebe continues, "and have, like, no money this year maybe we".
Phoebe says, "could do Secret Santa. And then we each only buy one gift". She continues gesturing with her hands.
Phoebe adds, "and there's the added mystery. Know who gets who?" The others look toward her.
Rachel smiles slightly. Ross looks up from the cutting board.
Ross holds his knife and says, "Who gets whom."
Phoebe lowers her gaze, then glances back up, her lips parted. Ross looks down at the cutting board.
Ross looks down, still chopping celery, and says in a softer volume, "I don't know why I do that,"
Ross continues chopping the celery.
Ross says, "but it is correct," while looking at the celery.
Phoebe points her finger toward Monica.
Phoebe holds up a small piece of folded paper and says, "Hey, what's this?" Monica holds up a glass bowl and says, "It's all your names. Let's pick."
Monica shakes the bowl. The door opens, and Joey enters. Chandler turns his head toward the door.
Joey begins closing the door and says, "Hey, what's going"
Joey stops walking, looks at Chandler, then turns around and starts to walk back out the door.
Joey says, "on?" Monica extends her arm, gesturing for him to stay, and says, "Joey, come on. We're picking names for secret Santa." Phoebe grabs Joey's hand.
Joey allows Phoebe to pull him back inside and says, "Okay?"
Joey stands beside Phoebe, his hands at his sides.
Joey points to his chest and says, "I invented Secret Santa, you know?" Phoebe smiles.
Rachel and Chandler look at Joey. Rachel smiles slightly.
Joey looks at Chandler and says, "What are you laughing at?" Chandler replies, "You're the one who said Die Hard was your idea." Joey nods slightly and says, "It was."
Ross looks up from the cutting board, holding a knife.
Ross raises the knife slightly and says, "Don't make me show you the napkin."
Rachel points at Ross. The view transitions to a television screen displaying a Thanksgiving parade with a large turkey float moving down a street.
Parade band music plays from the television. The view shifts back to the apartment living room.
Rachel stands up from the back of the couch, stretching her arms, and says, "Oh, I'm going to take a nap." Chandler sits on the couch cushions.
Rachel continues walking past the couch and says, "Turkey makes me sleepy."
Monica looks toward Rachel while stirring the bowl and says, "We haven't eaten yet."
Rachel stops and turns her head back toward Monica.
Rachel says, "I know, but all that work you're doing to get it ready, and I" while looking at Monica, then turns away.
Rachel walks through a purple doorway, exiting the room.

In an open-plan apartment kitchen and living area, Chandler walks toward Phoebe, who is seated at a small round table, and Monica, who stands at a kitchen counter. Chandler points to Phoebe, then Monica, and says, "hey, by any chance, did either of you pick Rachel for your Secret Santa? Because I want to trade for her." Phoebe begins to answer, "I picked"
Phoebe stands up, dropping an item on the table, and raises her hands. "her. Oh, thank God, you want her," she says.
Chandler's eyebrows draw together as he looks at Phoebe.
"Wow. Why do you want to get rid of her so badly?" Chandler asks.
Phoebe gestures with both hands and says, "Because she exchanges every gift she ever gets. It's, like, impossible to get"
Phoebe clenches her hands into fists and bounces slightly as she says, "her something she likes. Come on, let's trade."
Chandler shakes his head and gestures with one hand. "Well, that's not true. That's not true. I got her that backpack, and she loved it. I remember how much she was" he says.
Chandler raises his hands as he continues his thought.
"crying the day that big dog ran off with," Chandler concludes, gesturing with his hands.
Chandler stops speaking and lowers his hands. Phoebe looks at him steadily.
Phoebe says, "Well, there was no big dog."
Chandler opens his mouth slightly, looking at Phoebe.
Chandler gestures with one hand and says, "All right, this sucks because I already got her this briefcase and I had RG."
Chandler traces letters in the air with his finger as he adds, "Put on it. Her initials."
Phoebe smiles slightly, then her expression settles.
Phoebe raises her shoulders slightly and says, "Okay, I'm really bad at initials." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler tightens his lips and looks downward.

Monica, wearing an apron, walks to the table holding a container and a knife. "Wow, you got your shopping done early this year," she says. Chandler puts his hands on his hips and replies, "Yeah, well, my Camel cash was about to expire."
Monica cuts a piece of food on a cutting board at the table.
Monica looks at Chandler and says, "Maybe you can give it to somebody else."
Monica gestures with her knife hand and asks, "Oh, like Ross Geller?" Chandler raises his hands and says, "Oh, you know what though?"
Chandler lowers his hands and says, "It's kind of a girly briefcase." Monica replies, "Who cares? He works in a museum."
Chandler nods, looking toward the refrigerator.
Chandler claps his hands together once and asks, "Hey, what time is it?"
Chandler points toward the living area, saying, "The big game's about to start."
Phoebe shakes her head and says, "You don't have to do that. Ross and Joey aren't here. You can watch the parade if you want."
Chandler points at her and says, "Thanks."
Chandler turns and walks toward the living area. Monica approaches the refrigerator.
Monica pulls the freezer door handle repeatedly. "What is wrong with this?" she asks.
Monica pulls hard on the handle; the door flies open. Monica grabs her eye with one hand and shouts.

Monica presses her hand to her eye, groaning. "Oh, my God. Ice just got in my eye," she says. Rachel enters through a purple door, raising her arms, and says loudly, "People are trying to sleep in here."
Rachel lowers her arms, looking toward the kitchen.
Chandler points toward Monica and says, "Monica got ice in her eye and it hurts." Phoebe leans in and says, "Open it up. Let me see."
Monica replies, "Oh, I can. It really kills." Chandler adds, "Well, maybe you should put some ice on."
Chandler bites his lip. Phoebe continues examining Monica's eye.
Phoebe pulls back and says, "Oh, it looks bad." Chandler says, "Honey, maybe we should take you to a doctor."
Monica replies, her hand still over her eye, "No, my eye doctor is Richard. I can't go to him when I don't have a"
"boyfriend," Monica finishes.
Monica keeps her hand over her closed eye.
Chandler leans forward and says, "He's really picky about his patients."
Audience laughter is audible as Monica shakes her head.
Phoebe puts a hand on Monica's shoulder and says, "You've got to go."
Monica keeps her head turned away slightly.
Chandler asks, "What's his office number?"
Monica lowers her hand from her eye slightly and says, "Like I remember his office number."
Monica rubs her eye with her hand.
Monica points briefly and says, "Speed dial seven."
Phoebe walks into the living area, picking up a cordless phone, and presses buttons on the keypad.
Phoebe holds the phone to her ear. "Hi. Yeah, I'm calling on behalf of Monica Geller's eye," she says.
Phoebe gestures with one hand and says, "And"
Phoebe lowers her gaze while holding the phone to her ear.
"is Richard Berg in today?" Phoebe asks. She listens, then says, "He's out of town."
Phoebe asks, "But does she want to see the on call doctor?" She looks toward Monica, points the phone slightly, and says, "Yes, she's very excited about"
"that," Phoebe finishes.
Phoebe listens to the phone and nods.
"Okay, we'll be right in," Phoebe says, then lowers the phone. Chandler asks, "Okay, so who's gonna take her in?"
Rachel points to herself and says, "I'll go. You can watch your parade."

Chandler jogs toward the living area, leaping over the back of an armchair and sitting down as the television plays. Outside, a brick building is visible. The scene then changes to the apartment kitchen where Phoebe stands at the table and Ross stands nearby holding a bottle.
Phoebe holds a small plate and a knife. "It's so cool. Till Monica gets back," she says.
Phoebe gestures with the knife and adds, "It's like I'm head chef and I get to make all the decisions."
Ross gestures toward Phoebe with his free hand.
Ross points at the table and says, "Hey, I thought she told you to follow the recipe exactly."
Phoebe points her thumb toward the doorway and says, "Okay, get out of my kitchen."
Ross raises his eyebrows and takes a sip from his bottle. Chandler enters through the front door, closing it behind him.
Chandler holds up one finger and says, "All right."
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "Okay, I think I am making some progress with Joey. When I went into the apartment, he went straight into his"
"bedroom. But he only slammed the door once," Chandler continues, holding up one finger.
Ross smiles slightly, looking at Chandler.
Chandler puts his hands on his hips and says, "I mean, yeah, he gave me the finger while doing it."
Ross drinks from his bottle as Phoebe looks at Chandler.
Phoebe lowers a hand mixer she is holding and says, "All right, I got to call my mom and ask her a left handed cooking."
Phoebe points with the hand mixer toward Ross and Chandler.
Phoebe gestures with her free hand and says, "Be in there"
Phoebe turns to walk toward the living area.
"while I'm gone," Phoebe finishes as she walks away.
Ross and Chandler remain standing in the kitchen area.
In a kitchen and living area, Chandler stands near a kitchen counter while Phoebe, wearing a patterned shirt, walks past him toward the living area.
Phoebe points a finger toward Chandler while holding a cordless phone and says, "Chandler, you're in charge." Chandler gives two thumbs up. She walks out of view as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler lowers his hands, his smile fading. He steps toward the living area, where Ross stands holding a dark bottle. Chandler stops near a small round table.

Chandler puts his hands in his pockets. Ross gestures with his free hand and says, "So listen, I picked Monica for Secret Santa but I'm already getting her something for Hanukkah. I was wondering if you want to"
Ross continues, "switch." Chandler removes his hands from his pockets, crosses his arms, and says, "Oh, you know what I was trying to trade for well, you," his pace fast. Audience laughter plays.
Ross points both index fingers at Chandler, smiling slightly.
Ross asks, "Really?" Chandler smiles broadly. Ross continues, "Wow, that's so nice."
Ross asks, "What are you going to get me?" Chandler, arms still crossed, says, "I don't know, RG. I was thinking something girly for your office." Audience laughter follows.
Ross's smile fades and he looks away.
Ross looks back at Chandler and says, "Yeah. Maybe Phoebe will switch with"

Chandler steps forward, uncrossing his arms, and interrupts, "no, no, you don't want to do that." Chandler steps closer, gesturing with one hand, and adds, "Then you're going to get stuck with Rachel" Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler continues, "and she exchanges every gift she ever gets." Ross gestures outward with his bottle hand, saying, "That's not true. I got her lots of stuff she never took back."
Ross continues, "Like that gold necklace I got her last year."
Chandler crosses his arms again, saying, "And when was the last time you saw her wear it?" Audience laughter is heard.
Ross looks slightly upward, saying, "Well, she wore it"
Ross continues, "all Christmas Day and then" his volume lowering. He gestures slightly with his free hand.
Chandler nods once, maintaining eye contact. Ross opens his mouth slightly, looking back at Chandler.
Ross lowers his gaze and says, "Big Dog," his voice quieter, followed by audience laughter.
The setting changes to a different apartment. A large cardboard box lined with bubble wrap sits on a coffee table. Joey places black table legs into the box. Ross walks in through the front door.
Ross steps further into the room, still holding the bottle, and stops. Joey grabs another table leg from the table and points at Ross.
Ross points toward the box and asks, "All this stuff that Chandler bought out of guilt?" Joey replies, "Everything," dropping the leg into the box and turning toward Ross.
Ross points behind Joey.
Ross asks, "Even the TV?" Joey turns to look back, then faces Ross again and says, "No, I'm going to put that in my room," gesturing toward the side. Audience laughter plays.
Joey walks toward the kitchen counter. Ross remains standing near the door.
Ross steps forward, saying, "Listen, Joey."
Joey grabs a microwave power cord.
Ross gestures with both hands and says, "I know what he did was wrong. But don't you think you could at least hear the guy out?" Joey crosses his arms.
Joey says, "Back when you and Rachel were together, if Chandler"
Joey gestures toward Ross and continues, "had kissed her, would you hear him out?" Ross nods slowly and replies, "It's a good point," as audience laughter is heard.
Ross rests one hand on the counter.
Ross asks, "So how long you to punish him?" Joey picks up the microwave. Joey replies, "Five years," turning away. Audience laughter plays.
Joey walks toward the cardboard box with the microwave.

Ross asks, "You've sentenced him?" Joey places the microwave in the box, crosses his arms, and points a finger at Ross, saying, "Hey, don't do the crime if you can't do the time." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey lowers his hand, keeping his arms crossed.
Ross says, "Joey, the guy's your best friend."
Joey holds up a hand, palm out, and interrupts, "Was my best friend." Joey gestures toward Ross and says, "Anyway, I don't know why you're pushing for him so hard with him out of the way"
Joey continues, "as my best friend."
Joey gestures to the empty space next to him.
Joey adds, "There's a spot open," pointing a thumb at his chest, followed by audience laughter.
Ross smiles, his eyes widening slightly.
Ross points to himself and asks, "Who, me." Joey smiles and nods as audience laughter plays.
Ross chuckles, his shoulders rising slightly.
Ross touches his chest and says, "Wow. I'm honored."

Ross points a finger and continues, "You know what I'm going to do as my first act as your best friend? I'm going to get you to talk to Chandler." Joey's smile fades and he crosses his arms. Audience laughter is heard.
Joey looks upward briefly and exhales audibly.
Joey looks at Ross and says, "All right,"
Joey shifts his weight slightly.
Joey raises an index finger at Ross and says, "but if you weren't my best friend" Audience laughter plays.
Ross laughs and pats Joey's chest. The setting changes to a coffee shop. Chandler sits at a small table, holding Kathy's hands across the table.
Kathy asks, "oh, my God. Is it really that bad?"
Chandler brings her hands toward his face and replies, "I walk into a room and he won't even talk to me. He just mumbles something in Italian," maintaining eye contact with her.
Chandler kisses her hands again.
Chandler adds, "And I know he only knows the bad words," lowering their hands back to the table. Audience laughter is audible.
Kathy looks at Chandler with her eyebrows drawn together.
Kathy says, "I'm so sorry. I feel responsible."
Chandler replies, "It's not your fault," his voice soft.
Chandler leans in and kisses Kathy. Joey walks into the coffee shop and stops near a tall green pillar.
Joey walks to the counter and asks Gunther, "Hey, Gunther, have you seen Chandler?"
Gunther replies, "I thought you were Chandler," looking at Joey. Audience laughter plays.
Joey pauses, his eyebrows raised. Gunther looks toward the seating area.
Gunther points and says, "but one of you is over there." Joey turns his head to follow his gesture. Audience laughter is heard.
Joey walks past the pillar and spots Chandler and Kathy sitting together at the table.
Joey stops and stares at them. Chandler is kissing Kathy.
Chandler pulls away, sees Joey, and exclaims, "Oh," sitting up quickly as audience laughter plays.
Chandler leans back slightly.
Chandler waves slightly and says, "hey, Joe," his voice thin.
Joey's jaw tightens.
Joey glares at Chandler, flicks his hand under his chin in an outward gesture, and says loudly, "Vafanapoli." Audience laughter is loud.
Joey turns sharply and walks out of the coffee shop. Kathy brings her hand to her mouth, her eyes wide. The setting changes to a waiting area with a couch and desk.
Two women sit on the couch. A man wearing a brown jacket opens the door and walks into the room, holding a briefcase and a white garment. He approaches a desk.
The man nods toward the desk and says, "Thanks for coming in." He turns toward the door.
In a waiting room, Rachel and Monica sit side-by-side on a gray couch. Monica holds a tissue to her right eye.
Rachel turns toward Monica and asks, "Oh, my God. How cute is the on call doctor?"
Monica lowers the tissue slightly and smiles.
Rachel holds a pen near her own eye and says, "So cute that I'm thinking about jamming this pen in my eye."
Monica lowers the tissue completely, smiling. Rachel touches Monica's arm and speaks, her words not clearly intelligible. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica holds the tissue near her mouth. Rachel continues speaking inaudibly, her facial expression animated.
Monica turns toward Rachel, gesturing with her left hand, and says, "Just best luck with eye doctors. I know"
The setting changes to a reception desk. A woman wearing a white lab coat sits behind the desk reviewing paperwork.
The receptionist looks up and says, "Dr. Burke will see you." Monica stands up rapidly, stepping forward with her hands raised, and says, "No, no, not Dr. Burke."
Monica gestures toward the receptionist with the tissue in her hand, saying, "Dr. Burke's out of town. The on call doctor will see me now."
The receptionist holds a piece of paper and replies, "Dr. Richard Burke is out of town. Dr. Timothy Burke, his son, will see you now."
Tim walks through a doorway beside the desk, wearing a white lab coat.
Tim asks, "Ready?"
Monica gasps and points at Tim with the tissue, her eyes wide. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica holds her hand to her ear in a phone gesture and smiles. The setting changes to an apartment kitchen, where Joey puts on a dark jacket beside a black duffel bag on the counter.
Chandler walks through the front door, leaving it open, and steps forward. He says, "Hey. I'm sorry."
Chandler gestures toward Joey with his right hand.
Chandler asks, "Where are you going?"
Joey adjusts his jacket collar.
Joey replies, "My folks," keeping his gaze down.
Chandler says, "Oh," raising his eyebrows.
Joey grabs the duffel bag by its handle.
Chandler asks, "When are you coming back?" Joey glances at him and says, "I don't know. Might stay there for a few days while I look for an apartment."
Chandler's eyes widen, and he steps forward.
Chandler asks loudly, "What?"
Joey lifts the bag and walks toward the living room area.
Joey says, "Yeah," walking past cardboard boxes.
Joey sets the bag on top of a pile of boxes.

Joey gestures with his hands and says, "At first I thought we could talk about this, work it out, but seeing you two together, I don't—" Chandler interrupts, stepping closer, and says, "hey, look, what"
Chandler holds his hands out, saying, "do you want me to say? Do you want me to say that I'll stop seeing her?" Joey shakes his head and replies, "It's not about her, okay?"
Joey continues, gesturing with his hand, "But seeing you two together just reminds me of what you did. And I don't want to live with someone who doesn't know what it is to be a"
Joey finishes, "friend. So let's see." He turns toward the boxes.
Chandler steps forward and says, "Look, I know what it is to be a friend."
Chandler adds, his voice softer, "I screwed up."
Joey nods, looking at Chandler, and replies, "Yeah, you did."
Joey grabs the duffel bag and says, "And that's why I'm leaving."
Chandler quickly steps closer, raising both hands.
Chandler gestures toward the hallway and says, his volume rising, "All right, look, if you're not going to stay for me, then at least stay for them,"
A white duck and a smaller white chick walk on the brown patterned carpet.
Chandler continues, pointing toward the birds, "okay? They have had a very difficult year. Well, with the robbery and all"
Chandler lowers his hand.
Chandler says, his pitch rising slightly, "that chick used to be very cute."
Joey turns his head slightly toward Chandler.
Joey gestures with his hand and says, "When that guy was robbing us when I was locked in the entertainment unit for like 6 hours, do you know what I was doing in there all"
Joey presses his hand to his chest and says, "that time? I was thinking about how I let you down."
Chandler stands with his mouth slightly open, looking at Joey.
Chandler says softly, "Wow. Yeah." Joey points at Chandler and says, "But if I didn't know him, what kind of friend you were going to turn out to be, I wouldn't have worried about it so"

Joey says, "much. See you around," and picks up the bag, turning away. Chandler grabs Joey's arm and says quickly, "All right. Wait. Come on. Wait. Just wait 1 second. There has to be something that I can do."
Chandler points to himself and says, "Something? If we still had that entertainment unit, I would get in there for 6 hours and think about how I let you"
Chandler gestures with his thumb over his shoulder and finishes, "download." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey stares at Chandler, then lowers his gaze, a slight frown visible.
Joey points his finger and says, "we got a box."
Joey and Chandler both turn their heads to look at a large wooden crate. The setting changes to a darkened medical examination room, where Tim holds an ophthalmoscope to Monica's right eye.
Tim, looking through the instrument, says, "I remember the last time I saw you."
Tim continues, "It was the morning I left for college"
Tim moves the instrument slightly around Monica's eye.
Tim says, "and you were just standing outside the Dairy Queen."
Tim lowers the instrument and steps back. Monica looks up at him and smiles.
Monica says, "Just probably waiting for it to open." Audience laughter is audible.
Tim smiles, slipping the ophthalmoscope into his coat pocket.
Tim looks at her and says, "Got to tell you, you look great." Monica smiles and replies, "Now you look great, too." Tim adds, "You're an excellent patient."
Tim turns away and walks toward a counter in the background.
Monica turns her chair to face him and asks, "So, how does it look?" Tim turns back and says, "Well, you've got a little scratch on your cornea."
Tim steps toward her and says, "You're going to have to wear a patch for a couple of days." Monica smiles broadly and says, "Like a pirate."
Tim pauses, his facial expression neutral as he looks at her.
Tim says, "That helps you."
Monica's smile fades slightly.

In an eye examination room, Monica sits in a patient chair, holding a tissue to her right eye. Tim, wearing a white doctor's coat, stands beside her. Both smile slightly. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica lowers the tissue and says, "So how long you been working with your dad?"
Tim smiles and nods slightly. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica brings the tissue back toward her eye and says, "Come on, one of us had to mention him."
Tim continues smiling and shifts his posture slightly.
Looking at Monica, Tim says, "I moved back here a couple of months ago."
Tim turns and walks toward a counter in the background.
Turning her head to follow his movement, Monica says, "Big family dinner tonight."
At the counter, Tim picks up a brown paper bag, turns back toward Monica, and shakes his head.
Holding the bag, Tim says, "No way."
Tim walks back to stand beside Monica's chair.
Tim reaches into the bag and pulls out a small plastic-wrapped item. He says, "I was going to have Thanksgiving at my girlfriend's, but we broke up."
Monica raises her eyebrows, parting her lips slightly, and says, "Oh,"
Audience laughter is audible. Tim lowers his gaze briefly before looking back at Monica.
Tim says, "she wasn't ready for a serious commitment."
Monica tilts her head and smiles as she emits a soft, high-pitched vocalization. Tim holds the small plastic-wrapped item.
Monica raises her tissue-holding hand slightly and says, "You made her walk the plank. Eye, matey." On the final phrase, she closes one eye and speaks out of the side of her mouth.
Tim smiles and looks at her.
Tim says, "You're not wearing the patch yet."
Monica's smile instantly vanishes. Her jaw drops and her eyes widen as she stares at Tim. Audience laughter begins.
Monica maintains her wide-eyed, open-mouthed expression, remaining still. Audience laughter continues until the segment ends.
